rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary: This class is a practical class that focuses on speaking English. The aim is for students to acquire practical English skills centered on speaking and listening skills and to be able to use English for real communication purposes. Students will do a variety of communicative tasks and activities based on various topics. In the process of using English to practice real-life communication, students will learn to participate in conversations and respond appropriately. This will develop international understanding about how language is used differently across cultures. The goal is to improve skills by practicing conversation in English in a wide range of language use situations. Students will develop English vocabulary, pronunciation skills, cultural use of language, and conversational techniques through this course.
aims: The purpose is to acquire the basic abilities of daily English conversation by communicating and making the best use of the students' English proficiency. English Conversation is a practical course in which students practice skills to express themselves in a foreign language and negotiate meaning in communicative conversational settings.
goals: Activate learned English and improve fluency.
Develop confidence in speaking about a wide range of topics.
Improve pronunciation with an eye towards international communication.
Build up useful vocabulary and natural expressions.
Develop an operational understanding of grammar, particularly spoken grammar.

plan.text:
Orientation/icebreaker
Going over lesson content and approach, course requirements and evaluation. Small group interaction. Playing games.

Topic 1. Getting to know you
Introducing yourself. Learning how to start a conversation. Practicing conversations used when you meet people for the first time. Practicing small talk.

Topic 2. Personality and ability
Describing personalities and qualities. Conversation about family and friends. Talking about strengths and weaknesses. Talking about childhood.

Topic 3. Food
Recommending a place to eat. Conversation about food preference. Conversation at a restaurant. Ordering food on the phone for delivery.

Topic 4. Movies
Talking about types/genres of movies. Conversation about favorite movies, actors, actresses, directors and music in movies.

Topic 5. Travel
Talking about destination. Conversation at the tourist information desk. Sharing travel experiences. Deciding what to take and pack. Talking about great souvenirs from each prefecture.

Topic 6. Stereotypes
Talking about various stereotypes. Conversation about regional and occupational characteristics. Discussing pros and cons of stereotypes.

Topic 7. Money
Talking about spending and saving money. Conversation about shopping. Discussing fundraising projects.

Topic 8. Countries and regions
Talking about different countries and cultures. Conversation about tradition.

Topic 9. Social Issues
Talking about some social issues and problems in Japan. Discussing how to solve the problems.

Topic 10. Summer vacation
Talking about vacation plans. Sharing the best summer memories. Discussing some good summer activities in your hometown. Talking about festivals and events in the summer.
Recap and preparation for the final exam

textbooks: All materials provided by the instructor.
prereq: This course is available to all first year students and is a requirement for graduation.
prep: Students should review and practice the vocabulary and phrases covered during the class.
teachers: ◎ ヘルムリンガー 麻美

extra.label: 区分1
extra.text: 平成20年度以降入学生／令和６年度以降入学生

extra.label: 区分2
extra.text: 共通教育）初年次科目／国際コミュニケーション科目基軸英語

extra.label: 履修における注意点
extra.text: １年次に必修。１外国語（英語）履修上限年間10単位。卒業にはⅠとⅡ両方が必要。

extra.label: 資格等
extra.text: 教免－省令で定める科目：外国語コミュニケーション

extra.label: この授業で身につける「10+1の能力」
extra.text:
人類の文化・社会・自然に関する知識
語学に関するリテラシー
表現力
コミュニケーション力

extra.label: オフィスアワーに関する補足
教員の実務経験の有無
授業形態
extra.text: 口頭での質疑やディスカッションを含む
